title: Design System: ARV Finance Data App

muted-foreground.note: Dark-mode value is hsl(220 9% 72%), tuned up from 65% for legibility. Owns DS.MUTED-FOREGROUND.

display.purpose: Page titles and large stat displays. One of only two roles that scale responsively.

title.purpose: Card headings (600) and financial values (700). The numbers go bold.

body.purpose: Primary text, spec rows, inputs, table cells. Prose caps 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Field names, links, button text, secondary headings.

elevation.name: hover-elevate
elevation.purpose: ~3% wash on hover for buttons, badges, list items. Painted on a ::after pseudo-element, not a shadow.

elevation.name: active-elevate-2
elevation.purpose: ~8% wash on press. The product has zero box-shadows by design (DS.NO-SHADOWS).

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default for hover/active/color state changes. Motion conveys state, never decoration.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main CTA. Confident, cyan, not loud. Uses brightness on hover/press (not the overlay) because a wash over a saturated fill reads muddy.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M5 12h14"/><path d="M12 5v14"/></svg>Post a deal</button>

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Alternative action. Neutral fill, hairline outline, hover lands on the accent surface.

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Low-emphasis, contextual. Transparent with a faint outline.

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Text field. Input border is deliberately darker than other hairlines. Focus is the Radix ring, never a hand-rolled outline.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="Search transactions by MSA, address, company" />

components.name: Badge
components.description: Compact status/label chip. Always rounded-md, 12px semibold, never wraps.

components.name: Card
components.description: Panel surface. Flat at rest; deal cards take a cyan border on hover/select. No shadow.

components.name: Sidebar Item
components.description: Primary nav row. Active row is the one place brand cyan appears in the chrome.
components.html: DataDeals

narrative.overview: ARV Finance is a lender, and this app is the workspace it hands its borrower-clients — wholesalers and fix-and-flip operators running deals across a handful of active MSAs. The visual system has to earn the trust of a paying client before it impresses them with data, and then do both at once: the trusted insider held together with the sharp analyst. The Insider's Desk is that room — uncluttered, credible, everything a pro needs within reach, nothing shouting for attention. Visually this reads as restraint in service of confidence: a near-white canvas (deep neutral in dark mode), a single cyan brand accent used only where it means something, and one well-tuned sans (Inter) carrying every heading, label, value, and row. Depth is quiet — no drop shadows anywhere; surfaces are flat at rest and lift only on interaction. Density is calibrated per surface: Data searches like Zillow/Redfin, Mastermind works like Slack, and all four apps are plainly the same product.
keyCharacteristics: One accent (cyan 192 67% 65%), spent only on meaning: CTAs, active state, focus, links, primary series.
One family, Inter, at a fixed rem scale — hierarchy from weight and size, not fluid scaling.
Flat by default; depth is a hover/active overlay, never a shadow.
Neutral-cool grays throughout; warmth is deliberately absent (no cream, no beige).

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Insider Cyan is the only brand color, and it appears only where it carries meaning — action, selection, focus, link, primary series. Its rarity is what makes it read as authoritative.

rules.name: The Cool-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Grays carry a faint blue cast (hue 220), never a warm one. No cream, no beige, no sand — those are the generic-template tell this brand rejects.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Type sizes are fixed, not fluid. Exactly two things scale with the viewport — page titles and a growing card heading. Clamp-sized headings belong to the marketing layer, never app UI.

rules.name: The 12px Floor Rule
rules.body: text-xs (12px) is the minimum for UI text, exceptions being avatar initials and the dense transaction list. Smaller means the container is too small.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Elevation is a response to state (hover, press, toggle), never ambient decoration.

rules.name: The Overlay-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Never reach for a box-shadow to separate surfaces — use the border ladder or the elevate overlay. An overflow:hidden ancestor clips the overlay; fix the clip, don't add a shadow.

dos: Do spend Insider Cyan (192 67% 65%) only on meaning — action, selection, focus, link, primary series. Neutral everything else.
Do route every secondary text through text-muted-foreground (DS.MUTED-FOREGROUND); never a hand-picked gray.
Do convey depth with the elevate overlay or the border ladder, never a shadow (DS.NO-SHADOWS).
Do hold type sizes fixed; scale only page titles and growing-card headings (DS.FIXED-TYPE-SIZE).
Do use a semantic token for every color; the only hex allowed is in the four sanctioned categorical palettes (DS.NO-HARDCODED-COLOR).
Do match density to the surface — Data can run tight like Redfin, Mastermind reads like Slack.
donts: Don't ship the generic AI/SaaS template: no cream/beige backgrounds, no gradient hero, no identical icon-heading-text card grids, no tiny tracked-uppercase eyebrow over every section.
Don't drift toward the cluttered legacy real-estate portal: no banner-ad density, no cramped MLS-style tables.
Don't go cold enterprise/bank: no sterile corporate chrome that feels like a mortgage form. Credible, not clinical.
Don't go consumer-toy: no cartoonish illustration, no over-animated motion, no unserious flourishes.
Don't warm the neutrals. Grays stay cool (hue 220); a warm-tinted surface is off-brand.
Don't add a second font family, a box-shadow, or a fluid clamp heading to app UI.
